coin_profile.name: Venice Token
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: Venice Token (VVV) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #81 by market cap.

VVV.name: Venice Token

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

moat.rationale: VVV is a cash-flow protocol with moderate fundamentals across all quality signals (mostly 4-6), indicating neither strong competitive advantages nor network effects. As a relatively young protocol (maturity score 4) with limited ecosystem lock-in and forking risks typical of cash-flow models, it lacks the defensibility of settlement layers or established DeFi primitives; token holders capture some value but dilution control is weak (4/10), suggesting limited long-term moat sustainability comparable to governance tokens like ARB/OP rather than core infrastructure.

chart_zones.mos_note: Cycle phase (Distribution (near ATH)) not favourable for entry — wait for accumulation or early uptrend.

2026.net_issuance_note: Current snapshot only. Historical supply data requires on-chain indexing not available via free APIs.

meta.data_note: No protocol fee or TVL data found for this token on DeFiLlama. Charts 1 and 2 are unavailable. Charts 3 and 4 show current-year supply and dilution data from CoinGecko.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 4.7/10 and survivability 43.3%. Base: 3.0% p.a., Bull: 9.0% p.a., Bear: -37.0% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (3.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (4.7/10). Bull (9.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-37.0%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 43.3% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Metcalfe Network Value is shown in the breakdown chart without contributing to the median: the implied IV is below 10% of current price, indicating the token is trading at a significant premium to its network activity level. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment penalises the large gap between circulating and fully-diluted supply — future token unlocks are expected to create selling pressure. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.
forecast_meta.price_history_note: Limited price history available (1 year(s)). This token may be recently listed or have insufficient on-chain data.

VVV.iv_method: Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, Dilution Adjustment

coin_profile.description: VeChain (VET) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #99 by market cap.

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

moat.rationale: VET has a niche enterprise moat through established supply chain partnerships (Walmart China, DNV) and custom blockchain infrastructure, but lacks crypto-native network effects or meaningful token value capture—holders receive minimal fee accrual while the protocol remains dependent on a small set of authority nodes, limiting true decentralization. The ~99% circulating supply and mediocre scores across fee growth, capital efficiency, and holder value accrual indicate weak economic defensibility; competitors could fork the tech or enterprises could build custom solutions, while regulatory risk from China exposure further erodes moat strength.

chart_zones.mos_note: Margin of safety present — price near or below speculative fair value during accumulation (deep bear) phase.

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control

moat.rationale: VIRTUAL demonstrates weak protocol moat characteristics: extremely low protocol maturity (2/10) suggests nascent technology vulnerable to forking, minimal holder value accrual (5/10) indicates token economics don't effectively capture cash flows, and dilution control concerns (4/10) threaten long-term incentive alignment. While network demand scores exceptionally high (10/10), this reflects early adoption momentum rather than structural defensibility—the protocol lacks the 15-year security history of settlement layers, established ecosystem lock-in, or irreplaceable network effects that would create durable competitive advantages against well-funded competitors.

coin_profile.name: Velo
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: Velo (VELO) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #404 by market cap.

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

moat.rationale: VELO is a Solidly fork operating on Optimism with minimal competitive defensibility—the AMM+ve(3,3) model has been replicated across multiple chains without meaningful differentiation. While it captures some protocol cash flows through ve-tokenomics, the pipeline scores reveal weak fundamentals across growth (5), efficiency (5), and holder value (5), with critically low maturity (1), indicating nascent product-market fit and high execution risk. Network effects are limited to OP's ecosystem; switching costs are near-zero given commodity liquidity provision, and regulatory resilience is hampered by the protocol's concentrated governance and dependency on Optimism's regulatory trajectory.

chart_zones.mos_note: Price above speculative fair value — no margin of safety.

2026.net_issuance_note: Current snapshot only. Historical supply data requires on-chain indexing not available via free APIs.

meta.data_note: No protocol fee or TVL data found for this token on DeFiLlama. Charts 1 and 2 are unavailable. Charts 3 and 4 show current-year supply and dilution data from CoinGecko.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 4.4/10 and survivability 40.0%. Base: 3.0% p.a., Bull: 9.0% p.a., Bear: -37.0% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (3.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (4.4/10). Bull (9.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-37.0%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 40.0% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Metcalfe Network Value is shown in the breakdown chart without contributing to the median: the implied IV is below 10% of current price, indicating the token is trading at a significant premium to its network activity level. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment did not fire: FDV/MC is within the acceptable range for this quality tier, so no dilution penalty was applied. Only 2 methods contributed to the median. More data sources (DeFiLlama fee history) would improve confidence in this estimate. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.

moat.rationale: VVS is a forked DEX on Cronos with no defensible moat—it faces intense competition from superior protocols (Uniswap, other L1 DEXs) and users can easily migrate or fork the code. The pipeline scores reveal critical weaknesses: minimal network demand (1), poor dilution control (2), zero protocol maturity (1), and despite being classified as a cash-flow protocol, holder value accrual is only 5/10, indicating token holders capture negligible real economic value. With low market cap ($0.1B), concentrated supply (43.55% circulating), and no ecosystem lock-in on a secondary L1, VVS has virtually no switching costs or competitive advantages.
